wine_festivals.name: Essencia do Vinho Porto
wine_festivals.recurrence_pattern: annual, last weekend of February into early March
wine_festivals.host_organization: Essencia do Vinho / Revista de Vinhos
wine_festivals.description: Portugal's flagship wine fair fills the neoclassical Palacio da Bolsa in Porto with around 4,000 wines from more than 400 producers, Douro reds and Ports prominent among them, across four days of tastings and masterclasses.
wine_festivals.tip: Saturday is the busiest and dearest day; a weekday afternoon ticket gives you far more room at the producer tables.

wine_festivals.name: Festa de Sao Joao do Porto
wine_festivals.description: Porto's midsummer street festival on the night of 23 June, celebrated for more than six centuries, fills the city with grilled sardines, bonfires, plastic hammers and free-flowing Vinho Verde and Douro wine until dawn.
wine_festivals.tip: The riverside Ribeira and the Fontainhas neighbourhood are the liveliest; base in Porto for the night, then escape to the quiet valley to recover the next day.

wine_festivals.name: Douro Vindima harvest experience
wine_festivals.recurrence_pattern: annual, mid September to early October
wine_festivals.description: Across the vindima, quintas open the harvest to guests for grape picking on the terraces, traditional foot-treading in granite lagares after dark and long harvest feasts. It is the most atmospheric time of the Douro year.
wine_festivals.tip: Choose an estate that still treads by foot for the full lagarada ritual; the popular September dates sell out by late summer.

wine_festivals.name: Douro Wine Festival, Peso da Regua
wine_festivals.recurrence_pattern: annual, late August around the start of harvest
wine_festivals.description: Regua's harvest-season wine festival fills the riverside town with tastings of local Port and Douro table wines, regional gastronomy, grape-stomping demonstrations and live music against the terraced slopes of the valley.
wine_festivals.tip: It coincides with the start of the harvest, so pair it with a quinta visit nearby to watch picking and fermentation begin.

wine_festivals.name: Sao Martinho magusto, new-wine and chestnut feast
wine_festivals.host_organization: Towns and villages across northern Portugal
wine_festivals.description: Around Saint Martin's Day on 11 November, villages light bonfires, roast chestnuts and tap the first young wine of the harvest in the magusto feast. The wine-growing north, including the Douro and neighbouring Tras-os-Montes, keeps the tradition most strongly.
wine_festivals.tip: Seek out village tascas serving agua-pe and jeropiga alongside roast chestnuts, the rustic part-fermented drinks of the new-wine season.
